action.reasoning: Observation: ConvNeXt-Tiny 224px+mixup+LS for 6 epochs got 0.7709, slightly worse than 3-epoch baseline 0.7743. Both very close. Analysis: With 40% noise, model is at risk of memorizing wrong labels with more epochs. Best strategies for noisy labels: (1) early stopping, (2) sample selection - use small-loss samples (Co-teaching/CleanLab), (3) ensemble. With 4.7h left, I should try a more powerful approach: use cleanlab to identify clean samples or use a confident-learning approach. Or: use combined train+val with noisy labels, then early stop. Better: train ConvNeXt-Tiny carefully tuned + use TTA at inference. Or use larger model. Decision: Try a cleaner approach: train ConvNeXt-Tiny with strong augmentation, early stop based on training dynamics. Use ensemble of 2 short runs at different seeds + TTA (horizontal flip averaging). First train one solid model (4 epochs at 224 with mixup+LS, but lower LR, using both train+val data combined since val labels also noisy = more data). Save & submit eval.
